description: Generate non-overlapping balls. The process ceases when the target number of balls are created or when the number of attempts to place balls without overlap is achieved. This command contrasts with ball distribute where balls are distributed without regard for overlap until a target porosity is achieved.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: itasca.command('ball generate ...') - Python SDK only has itasca.ball.create() for single ball creation

keywords.name: box
keywords.description: Ball will fully fall within this box, such that they will not overlap the box faces or edges. By default, the box is the model domain. Following specification of the box extent in the \(x\) -direction, if subsequent extents are omitted, then the latest specified extent is used.

keywords.name: cubic
keywords.description: The ball positions are assigned so that a regular, cubic packing results that entirely fills the specified region. The lowest radius, fradlow , is used for the ball radii so that the ball centroids are 2* fradlow units apart in each axial direction.

keywords.name: fish-distribution
keywords.description: The ball radii are drawn from the distribution represented by the FISH function s . Function arguments can be specified as though no function arguments are required. The FISH function must return a floating point value that is the ball radius. A FISH distribution cannot be specified with the cubic , gauss , hexagonal , or radius keywords.

keywords.name: gauss
keywords.description: Specify that the ball radii are chosen from a Gaussian distribution with mean value ( fradlow + fradhi )/2 and standard deviation ( fradhi - fradlow )/2. The optional f (default 0.1), multiplied by fradlow , constitutes the minimum radius allowed. Cannot be given with the cubic or hexagonal keywords.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Specify that generated balls are given group name s at slot slot . If the slot keyword is not specified, then the group name is assigned to the slot Default .

keywords.name: hexagonal
keywords.description: The ball positions are assigned so that a {hexagonal in 2D; face-centered-cubic in 3D} packing results that entirely fills the generation region. The upper radius, fradhi , is used for the ball spacing. The ball radius is chosen from a uniform distribution with minimum radius fradlow and maximum radius fradhi , is used for the ball radii.

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: The ID of the first ball generated is set to idlow that, by default, is the next available ID. idhi , if specified, is used to calculate the number of balls to generate. Ball IDs are chosen sequentially. The specification of idhi does not affect the cubic or hexagonal behaviors.

keywords.name: number
keywords.description: Specify the number of balls to be generated. The specification of i does not affect the cubic nor hexagonal behaviors.

keywords.name: radius
keywords.description: Specify the radius range to be used during generation. If fradhi is not specified then fradhi = fradlow . By default, fradhi = fradlow = 1.0. Cannot be given with the fish-distribution keyword.

keywords.name: tries
keywords.description: Number of tries to fill the specified region with the number of balls. By default, i = 20,000.

examples.description: Generate 100 balls with radii 0.5-0.7 in left half of domain (non-overlapping)
Generate cubic packing with ball radius 0.25 (spacing = 0.5)
Generate hexagonal close packing with radii 0.3-0.5

examples.command: ball generate radius 1.0 1.5 gauss 0.1 number 500 tries 50000
examples.description: Generate 500 balls with Gaussian distribution, up to 50,000 attempts
Generate balls with IDs 1-500, assign to group 'layer1'
